extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is an inductive invariant, so the bad state (opted_out=true AND sent=true) is unreachable. Proof by checking each transition preserves the invariant: (1) sent becomes true ONLY via t2, whose guard requires not opted_out, and t2 leaves opted_out=false, so the implication is vacuously satisfied after t2. (2) opted_out becomes true ONLY via t1, which simultaneously sets sent:=false, so the post-state has sent=false. (3) t3 (send_attempt while opted_out) sets sent:=false. (4) t4 (tick) sets sent:=false. The initial state has opted_out=false (vacuously satisfies the invariant). No transition can set sent=true while opted_out is/becomes true, and no transition sets opted_out=true without clearing sent. Thus opted_out=true always co-occurs with sent=false in every reachable state, for executions of any length including up to 8 events. The property holds.
